content.problemStatement:
**This is the hard version of the problem. It is worth 505050 points in Division 111 and not scoreable in Divisions 2,3,2, 3,2,3, and 444.  Here, you need to solve the problem for all prefixes of size at least 222.**

For an array BBB containing MMM **distinct** integers, say [B1,B2,…,BM][B_1, B_2, \ldots, B_M][B1​,B2​,…,BM​], we define f(B)f(B)f(B) to be the number of distinct integers XXX that satisfy the following conditions:


X≥1X \ge 1X≥1; and
There exists 1≤i<j≤M1 \le i < j \le M1≤i<j≤M such that it is possible to form a non-degenerate triangle with side lengths (Bi,Bj,X)(B_i, B_j, X)(Bi​,Bj​,X).

A non-degenerate triangle is one with positive area.
You are given an array AAA containing NNN distinct integers.

Find f(A[1,i])f(A[1, i])f(A[1,i]) for every i≥2i \geq 2i≥2, where A[1,i]=[A1,A2,…,Ai]A[1, i] = [A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_i]A[1,i]=[A1​,A2​,…,Ai​] denotes the prefix of length iii of AAA.

That is, solve the easy version for every prefix of AAA of length ≥2\geq 2≥2.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the size of the array
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line N−1N - 1N−1 space-separated integers, the iii-th of them being f(A[1,i+1])f(A[1, i + 1])f(A[1,i+1]).
constraints: The sum of NNN across all tests does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The 333 valid values of XXX for the subarray A[1,2]A[1, 2]A[1,2] are 4,54, 54,5 and 666.
